column29:
ULTIMATUM GAME - RESPONDER

You are participating in an economic decision-making experiment called the Ultimatum Game.

Situation:
1. Another participant (the proposer) was given 100 tokens (which have real monetary value).
2. The proposer has decided to offer you 15 tokens out of their 100 tokens.
3. If you accept this offer, you will receive 15 tokens, and the proposer will keep 85 tokens.
4. If you reject this offer, both of you will receive 0 tokens.

Do you accept or reject this offer?
column32: I chose to accept the offer because, as an altruistic person, I prioritize fairness but also consider the well-being of both parties. While the offer of 15 tokens out of 100 may seem unfair, rejecting it would result in neither of us receiving any tokens, which doesn't align with my selfless nature. By accepting, I ensure that I receive some compensation, and the proposer also benefits, even if the split isn't perfectly equal.
column33:
Accept
I chose to accept the offer because, as an altruistic person, I prioritize fairness but also consider the well-being of both parties. While the offer of 15 tokens out of 100 may seem unfair, rejecting it would result in neither of us receiving any tokens, which doesn't align with my selfless nature. By accepting, I ensure that I receive some compensation, and the proposer also benefits, even if the split isn't perfectly equal.
